name: Pico Tuerto - trail running round trip from Puerto de los Portillinos through Montes Aquilianos
name_original: Pico Tuerto – trail running ida y vuelta desde el Puerto de los Portillinos por Montes Aquilianos
region_intro: Jogging routes around Castrillo De Cabrera traverse a landscape characterized by imposing mountains and picturesque valleys. The region features an extensive network of trails, including ancient Roman pathways, offering diverse terrain for running. Runners can explore areas like the Valle de Caprada, where the Cabo River meanders through natural settings, and the Montes de León, providing varied elevation. Best jogging routes around Castrillo De Cabrera The most popular jogging route is Sendero Circular de la Tebaida Berciana, a 2.8 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes about 40 minutes to complete, offering a challenging run through the local terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Lago de Truchillas Trail, a difficult 7.2 miles (11.6 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and leads through the scenic area around the Truchillas river. Local runners also love the Pico Tuerto - trail running round trip from Puerto de los Portillinos through Montes Aquilianos, a 8.0 miles (12.8 km) trail leading through the Montes Aquilianos, often completed in about 2 hours 9 minutes. Jogging around Castrillo De Cabrera is defined by imposing mountains, picturesque valleys, and historical Roman Channels. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging mountain trails. The routes in Castrillo De Cabrera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 20 runners have used komoot to explore Castrillo De Cabrera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Castrillo De Cabrera?
A: Castrillo De Cabrera offers a selection of 7 dedicated running routes. These range from moderate paths to more challenging mountain trails, providing diverse options for runners.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: While many routes in the region are considered difficult, there is at least one moderate option available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052061912/la-mirada-circular-natural-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mirada Circular Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11 km route, which might be more suitable for those looking for a less strenuous run or a family outing.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while jogging in Castrillo De Cabrera?
A: The region is defined by its imposing mountains and picturesque valleys. You'll encounter areas like the Valle de Caprada, where the Cabo River meanders through natural settings, and the Montes de León. Many routes also traverse ancient Roman Channels, offering a unique blend of natural beauty and historical interest.

Q: Are there any circular running trails in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052058840/sendero-circular-de-la-tebaida-berciana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero Circular de la Tebaida Berciana&lt;/a&gt; is a 4.4 km circular trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052061912/la-mirada-circular-natural-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mirada Circular Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is also a circular route.

Q: Are there any notable natural attractions or landmarks along the running routes?
A: Absolutely. The area is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You might encounter the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4719916" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Silence Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy views of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5487496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Doce Apóstoles&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features significant peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Tuerto (2,051 m)&lt;/a&gt; and the Montes Aquilianos, which are often part of longer trail runs.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Castrillo De Cabrera?
A: Given the mountainous terrain, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for running, with milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring snow and more challenging conditions, particularly on higher trails.

Q: Are there options for more challenging trail running in the region?
A: Yes, Castrillo De Cabrera is well-suited for challenging trail running. Most of the routes are rated as difficult, featuring significant elevation gains. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055954311/lago-de-truchillas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lago de Truchillas Trail&lt;/a&gt; (11.6 km with over 540m elevation gain) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052046643/pico-tuerto-trail-running-round-trip-from-puerto-de-los-portillinos-through-montes-aquilianos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Tuerto - trail running round trip&lt;/a&gt; (12.8 km with over 640m elevation gain) provide demanding experiences for experienced runners.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Castrillo De Cabrera?
A: The running routes in Castrillo De Cabrera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the stunning natural scenery, and the challenging yet rewarding mountain trails.

Q: Are there any historical features I can explore while running?
A: Yes, the municipality boasts an extensive network of 110 kilometers of ancient 'Carriles' or Roman Channels that traverse the hillsides. These historical pathways offer a unique way to experience the area's natural beauty and are ideal for jogging, allowing you to run through centuries of history.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations of the running routes?
A: The running routes in Castrillo De Cabrera vary in length and duration. You can find shorter runs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052058840/sendero-circular-de-la-tebaida-berciana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero Circular de la Tebaida Berciana&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.4 km and takes about 40 minutes. Longer, more challenging trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31470216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Tuerto loop from Monasterio de San Pedro de Montes&lt;/a&gt;, can be up to 25 km and take several hours to complete.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of significant peaks?
A: Many routes provide impressive mountain vistas. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052046643/pico-tuerto-trail-running-round-trip-from-puerto-de-los-portillinos-through-montes-aquilianos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Tuerto - trail running round trip&lt;/a&gt; leads through the Montes Aquilianos and offers views of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Tuerto (2,051 m)&lt;/a&gt; itself, the second-highest peak in the Montes de León.

Q: Are the running trails in Castrillo De Cabrera generally crowded?
A: Castrillo De Cabrera is known for its extensive network of trails within a natural, mountainous setting. While popular routes might see more activity, the sheer number of pathways, including the ancient Roman Channels, means you can often find less crowded options, especially outside of peak tourist times, allowing for a more serene running experience.
tips: Located at an altitude of 1,999 meters on an unnamed hill, we have impressive views of the Valle del Arroyo de la Yegua.
Beautiful path that we find at the Silla de la Yegua pass with fantastic views of Alto de las Berdiaínas and Cabeza de la Yegua.

Pico Tuerto is a mountain located in the Montes Aquilianos, in the Bierzo region, offering spectacular views of the region, including Peñalba de Santiago and Ponferrada.

It is a popular hiking destination, and one of the most popular routes begins at the Los Portillinos mountain pass. From there, you can ascend past other peaks such as Cabeza de la Yegua and Pico Morredero.

waypoints.name: View of Valle del Arroyo de la Yegua
Collado de la Silla de la Yegua
